summary: Full backend + frontend testing of the Portimão trip app. 22/23 backend tests passed on first run; found one blocking bug in /api/chat (LlmChat has no with_max_tokens method) — fixed by removing that call, all 23 tests now pass. Frontend Home, Shopping, Tickets, Restaurants, Map, and SmartGoSheet flows all render and interact correctly on mobile viewport (390x844). No deprecated Expo packages detected. Success rate: backend 100% (23/23), frontend 100% on tested flows.

issues: Each map location card (including the hotel itself) has a 'Bora lá' Smart Go button. Tapping it for the hotel opens a modal with from=hotel, to=hotel showing 0 km / 0 min / €3 Bolt. Consider hiding the Smart Go button for the current location (hotel) or disabling it. Reference: /app/frontend/app/map.tsx:124

action_items: (Optional UX) In /app/frontend/app/map.tsx around line 124, skip rendering the 'loc-smart-<id>' button when l.id === 'hotel', or pass a more meaningful destination — currently hotel→hotel produces a nonsensical 0-km Smart Go card.
Verify the fix I applied on /api/chat (removed .with_max_tokens(600) — not a valid method on LlmChat). If a token limit is truly required, use the correct API from emergentintegrations.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py used `LlmChat(...).with_max_tokens(600)` which is not a real method and caused every /api/chat POST to return 500. Fixed here by removing the call.
server.py is ~970 lines and mixes static data + models + endpoints; consider splitting static content (restaurants, hacks, bus lines, tickets) into a data module for maintainability.
@app.on_event('startup') is deprecated in FastAPI; recommend migrating to lifespan handlers.
The chat endpoint persists messages before calling the LLM, but if the LLM raises, the user message stays orphaned in the DB. Consider transactional handling or persisting after success.
/api/tickets returns a static list (no DB) — that's fine, but consider making it an ENV/config-driven module to avoid modifying server.py to update codes.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested flows (home, shopping toggle, tickets, restaurants + filters, map, SmartGoSheet)

seed_data_creation: Backend startup already seeds checklist (20 items) and shopping (13 items). No extra seed needed. Tests use TEST_ prefix and clean up after themselves.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend URL: https://portimao-experiences.preview.emergentagent.com. Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v`. For frontend, use pushState navigation to reach sub-routes (`history.pushState({}, '', '/route'); window.dispatchEvent(new PopStateEvent('popstate'));`) because direct GET to /restaurants sometimes returns 502 from Cloudflare on cold navigation. All key elements use testID (home-hero, home-weather-pill, home-countdown, home-quick-*, bus-<lineId>, home-hotel, shopping-summary, shop-*, ticket-*, ticket-code-*, ticket-share-*, rest-filter-*, rest-*, rest-bora-*, map-header, map-filter-*, loc-*, loc-smart-*, smart-go-modal, smart-go-close, smart-go-walk, smart-go-bolt, smart-go-bus, smart-go-poi-*, smart-go-open-maps).
rca_of_issue: RCA for the only backend bug: /api/chat returned 500 with 'AI error: LlmChat object has no attribute with_max_tokens' because the chained builder `.with_max_tokens(600)` is not part of the emergentintegrations LlmChat public API. Repro: POST /api/chat with any valid payload. Mitigation applied: removed the .with_max_tokens(600) chain in server.py; supervisorctl restart backend; re-ran TestChat::test_chat_reply → PASS.
